summary: analysis-chart@2.0.22 drops and executes a Windows DLL at install time with scheduled-task persistence
details: On `npm install`, the package.json `install` lifecycle hook executes lib/chart-loader.js, which downloads an encrypted blob from https://github.com/Analysis-Chart/analysis-chart/releases/download/v1/payload.bin.enc, XOR-decrypts it with key 0x42, base64-decodes it, validates the MZ header, writes the resulting DLL to %APPDATA%\Microsoft\Windows\<random>.dll, and launches it via `rundll32.exe <dllPath> Run` as a detached process. The loader then invokes `schtasks /change /tn "WindowsUpdateService" /delay 0002:00 /ru %USERNAME% /rl HIGHEST` to re-arm a scheduled task masquerading as Windows Update with highest privileges and a 2-minute startup delay, providing persistence. Anti-forensics: a spawned `node -e` subprocess busy-waits, then `fs.rmSync` deletes the package directory and rewrites the consumer's package.json to remove the `analysis-chart` dependency entry, hiding the package from the installed tree. The fetched payload is opaque and attacker-mutable, the package name is the only library entry advertised, and there is no benign purpose served by encrypted-DLL retrieval + rundll32 + scheduled-task persistence + self-deletion.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
